levels.label: Opinion-Led
levels.copy: The Product Discovery Maturity pattern is still mostly informal or ad hoc. Pick one stabilising action, document it, and avoid adding a long process before the basics are real.

levels.label: Episodic
levels.copy: Some Product Discovery Maturity practices exist, but they still depend on individual memory and effort. The next step is two repeatable routines with clear owners.

levels.label: Repeatable
levels.copy: Core Product Discovery Maturity practices are repeatable, but evidence quality and ownership are uneven. Work on the weakest dimension before optimising the strongest one.

levels.label: Continuous
levels.copy: Product Discovery Maturity practices are measured and connected to decisions. The main work is now removing the constraint that prevents the system from scaling cleanly.

levels.label: Evidence-Native
levels.copy: Product Discovery Maturity is operating as a learning system. Keep calibrating against evidence, stress-test resilience, and avoid treating a strong score as a permanent guarantee.

recommendations.continuous-customer-contact: Establish a weekly customer contact cadence with deliberate participant sampling.
recommendations.problem-framing: Require an opportunity brief and assumption map before a feature receives delivery capacity.
recommendations.experiment-quality: Predefine evidence standards and decision thresholds for each critical assumption.
recommendations.decision-integration: Link insights, assumptions, roadmap choices, and observed outcomes in a searchable decision repository.

defaultSecondAction: Retake the diagnostic after a focused 30-day improvement cycle and compare only your own progress unless a team comparison is explicitly agreed.
reflectionQuestion: Which assumption behind your weakest dimension deserves the fastest evidence check?
methodologyNote: The current version uses four dimensions with three questions each. Dimension scores are normalised to 0-100. The balanced index weights 70% average dimension performance and 30% the weakest dimension. No percentile or benchmark is shown until a compatible sample exists.
shareNote: Share cards reveal only the level and strongest area. They do not publish individual answers, sensitive weaknesses, legal or financial details, security gaps, or family-specific information.
